data.description: A Coaching class is much more than just teaching you, it's about guiding, helping, changing strategies according to different exams, profile building, preparing you for your interviews by taking mock sessions, and finally making you believe that you can and convert your respective calls. data.name: Radhesh Chandran
data.description: I enrolled for the CATKing - MICA online coaching sessions and I had a wonderful experience. The sessions were planned very well and the entire course contents were covered. Rahul Sir's enthusiasm is simply unmatched. I would definitely recommend CATKing.

data.name: Ammaar Golwala
data.description:
Honestly, the coaching guidance and methodology taken by the faculty at CATKing is of the top-notch level where people from top B-schools having cleared the same exam you preparing for give you a different approach and method altogether. Furthermore, to add to that the personalized mentorship offered is something I cherish the most and haven't experienced at any other coaching. I was never an extremely high-performing student hence, never got personalized attention from the mainstream coaching classes.
Yes at the end it's your sheer passion, motivation, and determination to get you through but the path to be taken has to be shown and guided by someone as awesome as the mentors who have been there and done that.

data.name: Pemiya Gandhi
data.description:
From personal experience, I was previously a Time student right at the point where I thought MBA is only for engineers and I was about to give up. I met up with CATKing mentors and let me tell you, they hear out your problems and will sit and work with you till you find yourself a solution. They'll always encourage you and make sure you reach your goal.
They don't just make you CAT-ready but Corporate ready as the learnings you'll acquire from Professionals who've not only studied in the best institute but also have worked with some top companies. Come down and meet CATKing mentors, you'll see the energy and time they put in for their students.

data.name: Kedar Bhat
data.description: CATKing helped me reach my dream of JBIMS. Rahul sir, along with the entire amazing faculty of renowned professors, guided me throughout the journey and especially till the college GDPI stage

data.name: Ishan Gangar
data.description:
When I enrolled for CATking, I was looking for something more than any other class would provide. I was looking for guidance and a structured process for preparing for a competitive exam, and I found exactly what I was looking for. Concepts and syllabus would be covered by any other coaching classes but CATking helps us plan our preparation of topics, tempo of preparation, and mocks.

Planning is very important in competitive exams where you have to study for a whole year and then the exam is just a matter of one day. A comprehensive plan for mocks and revision just a few months before exams offered by CATking was extremely crucial and effective. All I had to do was follow the plan.

Being experienced MBA professionals themselves Rahul sir, Anisha maam and Ekagra sir provided us with valuable insights from their experience before the interview rounds began. Their guidance also helped me identify key problem areas and techniques to improve time and speed during regular courses. I am extremely thankful to CATking and team, Rahul sir, Anisha ma'am, and Ekagra sir to help me get into one of the top Business Schools

data.name: Sakshi Gandhi
data.description: Thanks a lot CATKing for making my IIM Ahmedabad dream Come True! The mentoring truly ensured I beat all odds.

data.name: Vivek Kshirsagar
data.description: “I would like to thank Rahul sir for firing a spark in me to prepare for CAT 15 and go for MBA. I was not sure whether to go or not for MBA but the introductory session by Rahul sir motivated me and I decided to prepare for CAT 15. His quote 'Success is the best revenge' kept me motivated throughout the preparation for CAT 15 as I wanted to prove a point to someone.”

data.name: Samay
data.description:
Being a CA student I had no clue about the MBA system and how to prepare for MBA entrances, but the guidance provided by Catking helped me sail through the entire process. Their study material and mocks helped me to prepare for CAT in a structured manner, also the training given for group discussions and personal interviews was phenomenal. I would like to give a huge shout-out to all the faculties at Catking. Rahul sir is truly the king at
motivating students and he has even helped me improve my communication skills which will help me even in my MBA. Faculties like Ekagra sir, Atul sir, and Manoj sir have always been there to clear my doubts and queries. Would like to also mention Anisha Mam who was a constant at the Interview
preparations and always ready for any help. A heartfelt thank you to the entire Catking team.

data.name: Manmeet Kaur
data.description: I still remember the day, I came to CATKing just for a ray of hope or you may say a boost to work much harder. For a career-oriented person like me with a good profile, I took a year drop to give all the entrances. The results were decent but didn't live up to my own expectations and I am not those adjusting ones either. I always aimed for the best institute. So in Jan, I joined CATKing for my Cet entrance, and during that duration, I also gave micat entrance for MICA University - the best institute for strategic marketing and communication. Something just happens for a reason. I never thought of filling out the micat form, but the first time I met Rahul Sir he insisted the whole batch fill out the form and I did so. Micat is one of the most creative and interesting papers I ever wrote. Post the micat results, and I was shortlisted for the next round. For the ge-pi stage, CATKing made sure I was 100% prepared and grabbed that Prestigious SPOT OFFER. 2 days of intense ge-pi mock training from 9 am - 6 pm followed by 2 more sessions of doubt clearance. I was all set to crack the deal. On 20th march, I received a SPOT OFFER from MICA University and now I am a proud MICAN. My relationship with CATKing classes was only for 3 months but the learning I gained was immense and something worth all the money I invested. Thank you for CATKing team and Rahul sir :)

data.name: Sangharsh
data.description: "I would like to thank Rahul sir and the team for the motivation and shortcuts provided during my preparation. The base concepts were taught very meticulously which helped me build a strong foundation for all the competitive exams. My weak link in the verbal section was polished by sir which helped me gain confidence. Cheers to the team!! "

Walking on those guidelines I converted IMT Ghaziabad, XIMB and the CATKing speciality, MICA. So I definitely believe that CAT King will be beneficial in identifying and improving your weaknesses.

data.description: You will be in awe when you actually get to interact with faculty of such caliber. SP Jain, NMIMS, Havard Alumni - speaks volumes about the kind of guidance that you’ll be getting. One thing very crucial for any exam prep is personalized mentorship & continuous motivation and backing up, which helps you get through tough times. Rahul Sir and Anisha ma’am make sure that each and every student performs and gets through to top B-schools - not only with the entrance prep but also with GDPI “hacks”. data.name: Apoorva Choudhary
data.description: The best thing about CatKing is that each and every student gets personal attention here(which includes building up your profile too).Every student has his/her strength & weakness.Faculties at CatKing takes care that individually everyone’s weakness is worked upon and strength is strengthened more. USP at CATKING is Rahul Sir,who is a real ball of fire.Trust me ,the motivation which you will get from him,you won’t get it in any of the coaching in Mumbai(Take my words seriously).Anisha Ma’am’s personal guidance will make sure that you don’t lose the grip at any point of time Not only this ,CatKing has their teachers from SPJain,Harvard,Narsee Monjee and so on.. While preparing for PI,they just don’t make you ready to Rock PI,but they overall change your mindset and makes you a better version of yourself ,ready to enter the business world.

data.name: Sweta Patnaik
data.description: The point is institute selection shouldn't be only based on profiles but methods that teachers use to make their students get through a hard time of every entrance exam, going that extra mile to make sure that you don't flunk your chances in the interview. I don't have an idea how CL does that but having a short stint with CATKING I realized it personally values its students and makes sure u reach where u want to and I can say that coz despite of not being a regular student Rahul sir and Anisha mam did everything to make sure u get thru. Apart from being CAT-centric, it focuses on other institutes like MICA SPJAIN JBMIMS and their number speaks volume. They clearly own MICA and other B-schools.

data.name: Akshat Bhargava
data.description: The study material provided is more than enough for cracking almost all National Level Management exams. The online study portal with number of mocks give you a real exam feel. Another positive aspect of CATKing is that, unlike other institutions, they do not solely concentrate on CAT. They give due importance to various other exams like NMAT, MICAT, SNAP, CET, and International exams like GRE, GMAT to name a few. Apart from the experienced faculty, the students attending at any centre of CATKing is also quite good with students from varied educational backgrounds and mix of freshers with experienced professionals which creates a similar peer group as you will experience in a B-School. Once, you get a decent in any of the entrance tests, then you will experience an entirely different CATKing. The GD-PI trainings are the most rigorous and the most effective. In a GD or an interview, you have a cheat sheet ready to answer any questions thrown at you. The beat part is, whether you have an IIM interview or NMIMS, SNAP, SPJain interview, everyone is trained the same, in the same class with number of mock GD’s and mock PI’s. With everything said and done I would strongly recommend you to join CATKing!

data.name: Siddhant Jain
data.description: I had joined CATKing for GDPI course for the year 2022-23. In my opinion, it is a very comprehensive course for preparing for GD/PI processes of the top B-schools. The course is basically a mini-MBA or a pre-MBA covering topics like Marketing, Finance, Case studies and Current Affairs. Someone who is not following news regularly, current affairs' sessions really help in reducing anxiety of encountering questions in the interview/GD. You will also get to hear numerous interview experiences of all the B-schools from your peers. Also, current students/alumni of top B-schools will guide you. They will also give you tips for summer placements.

data.name: Sarojit Auddya
data.description: Post CAT, we all have to go through the seemingly endless journey of preparing “Current Affairs", basics of core concepts like finance and marketing as well as the procedure of writing in a Writing Ability Test which is required in every IIM interview. Prior to joining CATKing, I was clueless when it came to these preparations. CATKing's material is comprehensive and provides more than 200 WAT topics across different verticals such as current affairs, business related and lifestyle related to name a few along with functional knowledge dossiers ranging across topics such as finance, marketing, operations, product management, etc. Also, past interview experiences for all the top colleges are cataloged for us to go through. Thanks to CATKing, I managed to convert IIM B and IIM C!

data.name: Bhavesh Chaudhari
data.description: I joined CATKing last year for CAT 2022 just for last two months and from my personal experience, it certainly helped a lot in my prep to 99.44 %ile in it. Their dashboard has a wide variety of practice questions along with vast material for prep strategy. So even if you are planning for self prep, this dashboard content proves to be crucial in it. The mocks are also good considering their difficulty levels, as well as the live solving videos, and mock analysis videos which I felt were better than others. Coming to interview prep, I think that is the area where the dashboard content helps the most, with previous PI experiences, functional dockets, basic information on structuring your answers to college specific PI processes. Also with the connections you form during the WATPI period because of their huge community and considering the fees they charge, I think it was worth every penny I paid.

data.name: Sneha Baidya
data.description: I joined CATKing for their course. For me, it was extremely helpful. Regular classes alongside mock interviews helped me prepare better. The dashboard truly helped me a lot in terms of gaining knowledge as well as getting to know past interview experiences.

data.name: Somen Kalokhe
data.description: I gave CAT 2022, and being a CATKing student for about 11 months now i can say that I got a CAT 2022 - 99.62%ile SNAP 2022 - 98.6%ile XAT 2023 - 97%ile NMAT - 239. This is just a picture with Rahul Sir at a CATKing Toppers event after the results CATKing classes were a great and interesting learning experience because of exceptional faculty members. The Real CAT Mock test series was a very balanced series focusing on different sections furthermore was one of the closest to the level of the actual CAT exam. Other mock test series were equally helpful. Enough number of mocks are given to practice well. The Dashboard made it all easily accessible, with a progress and trophies section according to performance in a test. All the aspects of the preparation were covered by the resources and material available on the dashboard. One unique thing about them is how they keep the students motivated, Rahul Sir’s Daily YouTube lives was something we all waited for at the end of the day and also morning motivation videos were inspiring. Online classes require a higher degree of self-motivation and self-directed learning. As a student, you are responsible for managing your study schedule, setting goals, and staying motivated throughout the preparation journey. In my case, I did the hardwork and CATKing provided me with the direction and every resource necessary for me to get such results. I would like to end by saying that CATKing was totally worth it and you can see the same in their results.

data.name: Ali Sagar
data.description: I had joined CATKing classes . I would have to say it was worth it. Also, as a person who was new to SOPs and WATs, the sample materials they provided was of great help. Overall, was a good experience.

data.name: Vishal Shukla
data.description: CATKing is best place for CAT preparation. Their GDPI modules are extremely detailed and versatile, covering literally everything. And the mock interviews are taken by students/alums of premier institutes. So, you are getting interview experiences from individuals who have themselves given and cracked interviews of top institutions. So, i would highly recommend CATKing for interview prep. Trust me, they are unmatched in this domain. Now, moving on to CAT exam preparation. CATKing provides the whole package here as well. Classes, practice material, mocks, analyses of mocks. So yeah it's a good choice. Mocks are also very important, don't forget! CATKing classes are worth it.

data.name: Pritam Ghosh Adikari
data.description: I recently had the opportunity to enroll in CAT coaching at CATKing, and I can confidently say that it was a transformative experience for my CAT preparation. I am delighted to share that I have converted three IIMs and many IITs, which wouldn't have been possible without the guidance and support of the amazing team at CATKing. I have very weird sort of academics. 10 CGPA in 10th, 69% in 12th and 79% in BTech and 19 months workex that makes it a very weird combination for IIMs. Many would say repeat or maybe I could have done better but I am happy with the converts with this academics. My journey with CATKing began just 1.5 months before the CAT exam. I stumbled upon a YouTube video featuring Rahul sir, where he explained how CAT can be cracked by focusing on a specific number of questions. This perspective instantly instilled a newfound confidence in me, especially when I had almost given up on CAT, considering it to be one of the toughest exams for MBA. I will forever remain thankful to Rahul sir for his motivation and encouragement. Taking up courses at CATKing, I dedicated myself to intense preparation, investing around 8-10 hours per day for 1.5 months. The comprehensive study materials, well-structured classes, and knowledgeable faculty at CATKing played a pivotal role in my success. Their teaching methodology not only cleared my doubts but also provided a deep understanding of the concepts. Being a General Engineering Male (GEM) candidate, I was aware of the challenges associated with this category, as it is often given the lowest preference in the selection process. However, with the unwavering support of Rahul sir and the CATKing team, I was able to overcome these obstacles and secure a selection. I must mention that CATKing's approach to Data Interpretation and Logical Reasoning (DILR) was exceptional. Prior to joining CATKing, I had zero knowledge in this area and used to associate it with blood relation questions. However, the faculty at CATKing brilliantly demystified these concepts, allowing me to excel in DILR and the highest in that section. In conclusion, CATKing is an absolute game-changer for CAT preparation. The unwavering support, motivation, and guidance I received from Rahul sir and the entire team were invaluable. If you're serious about cracking CAT, I highly recommend CATKing as your go-to coaching institute. They not only make difficult concepts seem simple but also instill the confidence needed to excel in the exam.

data.name: Tushar Sarkar
data.description: "I was a student of CATKing and appeared for CAT 2022( 99.80 %ile). The overall ecosystem that CATKing tried to create for the journey of cracking CAT was great. I attended the online classes for a month or two and then started writing mocks and visiting the content that was available on the dashboard. The dashboard had videos on literally everything that CAT can possibly ask. In my opinion, the strength of CATKing lies in the accessibility of the resources needed for preparation. If you're an aspirant who can compliment this with your capabilities, then I would suggest you to go for CATKing. CAT is not a race, it's a marathon. Best of luck! I hope you convert the B-School of your dreams!"

But, having said that, a candidate also has to work hard because these interviews are very random and it definitely helps to have knowledge on a wide range of topics. data.description: Let me share my perspective on it. I started watching videos from the CATking channel years before as I wanted to improve my Verbal ability and know about the CAT exam. Now, as this was my first and last attempt for CAT I wanted to join preparation classes. However, I had time constraints as I was a working professional it was really difficult for me to join the CAT preparation classes anywhere so I took the path of self-study. You got to take this decision as per your situation, also you will need Mock Tests for sure I joined the GDPI batch of the CATKing after the CAT 2022 result. Now after the CAT exam and getting a decent %ile, I found myself bombarded by a lot of information that I was not able to manage. After finding out that I made some mistakes as not giving XAT, IIFT, NMAT, and SNAP exams and also didn’t fill out the MDI and SPjain forms I decided it is high time to join coaching, and as CATking was available as one of the best prices hence I joined it. There I did find a lot of resources that were present in the dashboard which could have helped me while preparing. Now for GDPI, you are required to talk to someone to get your thoughts clear for an interview, for this my mentor Komal Priya worked well for me and gave me enough confidence. For GDPI, I am sure that CATking will connect you with the right people and groups to help you out. In conclusion, I would say: You need to answer this question needs to be answered from your perspective and position. I do believe that CATking could have helped me to improve my however that might have come at the cost of practice sessions which could have been counter effective. data.name: M. Mishra
data.description: Long story short, if you're willing to work hard, YES! IT'S COMPLETELY WORTH IT. Brief background, I am a GEM (Genral Engineer Male), fresher, belonging to a tier 2 engg college, appeared for UPSC CSE (and failed the exam). d 92%ile plus in CAT, XAT 236 in NMAT, 98%ile plus in SNAP After the failure, I was completely dejected and felt as if I can not do anything worth while, I filled up the CAT form on the last date and searched for an online course, found CATKING, and taking a bet upon joined the course. (AND TRUST ME, IT WAS THE BEST DECISION I MADE) Exam was precisely 3 months away, I started with the induction program wherein we were taught how to use the dashboard. And once I got hold of it, there was nothing that could stop me, initially I used to attend live classes, but eventually I switched to the recorded sessions. Sudhanshu sir's Quant classes being my favorite, I marathoned every topic taught, and solved LOD 1 and 2 questions with it. Since I had less time on my side, I tried to divide my day in 3 parts, starting with Aoen essays (suggested and taught by Summit sir), jumping on to Quants and finishing with DILR. I simply followed strategy told by Summit sir, and took one of the section to the moon. In mid October I stared to give mocks for CAT, and completed 20+ full length mocks, and 20 sectional mocks in 45 days, giving almost one mock daily. Moinack sir's paper post mock analysis is a MUST, I learned most of the tricks just by seeing him solve mocks. CATKING also provides previous year CAT papers, and it's solutions at a single place which helped me save my time, and IT DOES HELP A LOT. Post CAT, I gave around 8–9 XAT mocks, and around 10 NMAT mocks. So it is what it is, and trust me, if you're willing to work hard, trust your intuition and give it a shot. data.name: Shivam Upadhyay
data.description: I will give my honest opinion about the classes and mba prep in general. little about me I got 99.68 %ile in my first attempt at CAT while working full time. I have also prepared earlier for GATE 2020 and GRE and TOEFL 2018. If you want short answer here are my two cents “you guys have an idea about your abilities and any coaching centre can not make you identify your weaknesses and strengths, you have to analyse the mocks, the classes and try to remain calm and collected during exam hour so first work hard on identifying these things then go for coaching classes to work on your weaknesses” Now the long answer, I joined the CATKING classes in aug 2022 after I took the decision to start seriously preparing for CAT. My first impression was that the classes are only for 40 minutes which is a great thing for a working person like me as I used to attend usually from office or while transit the 7–8PM class. I knew my weak point was quant and in dashboard I got a good amount of material to work with. I was just solving questions regularly of the concepts taught in the classes along with the Arun Sharma LOD 1 and LOD 2 which really helped. Sudhanshu sir was great and Rahul sir was the motivator and suggested good reading material for verbal. DI LR was okayish if you really struggle in DI and LR I would suggest keep solving arun Sharma and take the mocks as much as you can that’s what helped me during my prep. CATKING mocks were the only mocks I attempted so I have no idea about other coaching’s material and I guess in CAT the accuracy and mistakes minimising is the key which will only happen if you solve a hell lot of questions with speed. The Sunday’s mock solving session with Sudhanshu sir is awesome and we get to know what we are lacking in our way to solve the questions. overall my experience was good with the classes and my advice to all of you please don’t be just dependent on classes to make you clear any exam take some time out of your busy schedule and reflect on yourself to identify what you are good and bad at and work accordingly.
